entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: A legume crop essential for the livelihoods of poor farmers and rural economies in developing countries

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: The presence of aflatoxins in peanut seeds, which hampers international trade and adversely affects consumer health

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: The fungal organism responsible for causing aflatoxin contamination and production in peanuts

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The international research organization focusing on peanut aflatoxin research and breeding

entities.name: GENETIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: The inherent ability of peanut plants to resist preharvest seed infection and aflatoxin production

entities.name: PREHARVEST SEED INFECTION
entities.description: The infection of peanut seeds by A. flavus while the crop is growing in the field

entities.name: AFLATOXIN PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process by which A. flavus synthesizes aflatoxins within the peanut seed

entities.name: PRE AND POST HARVEST MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Agricultural methods used to minimize aflatoxin contamination during and after harvest

entities.name: BREEDING FOR AFLATOXIN RESISTANCE
entities.description: The process of developing peanut varieties with improved genetic resistance to aflatoxin

entities.name: IN VITRO SEED COLONIZATION
entities.description: The ability of A. flavus to colonize peanut seeds in a controlled laboratory environment

entities.name: G X E INTERACTION
entities.description: The interaction between genotype and environment that causes variable performance of resistance traits

entities.name: SCREENING PROTOCOLS
entities.description: The procedures used to evaluate and identify peanut lines with aflatoxin resistance

entities.name: GENETICS OF RESISTANCE
entities.description: The study and understanding of the genetic basis for aflatoxin resistance in peanuts

entities.name: ARACHIS GERMPLASM
entities.description: The collection of genetic material from cultivated and wild peanut species used for breeding

entities.name: TRANSGENIC RESEARCH
entities.description: The method of modifying the aflatoxin biosynthesis pathway or introducing antifungal genes to create resistance

entities.name: AFLATOXIN BIOSYNTHESIS PATHWAY
entities.description: The biochemical sequence of reactions that leads to the production of aflatoxin

entities.name: ANTIFUNGAL AND ANTI-AFLATOXIN PROPERTIES
entities.description: Properties of specific genes that inhibit fungal growth or the production of aflatoxins

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING
entities.description: Traditional plant breeding methods used to develop agronomically superior and resistant peanut varieties

relationships.source: PRE AND POST HARVEST MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
